title: Listen and Choose a Response — Practical Matters

questions.stem: Professor, would you mind signing my course withdrawal form?

options.A: Of course. Have you spoken to your academic advisor about this?
options.C: I had to sign many forms this morning.

questions.explanation: The speaker is requesting a signature on an official form, so granting the request while showing professional concern is appropriate.

questions.stem: I'm driving to the conference center. Do you need a lift?

options.B: I prefer to take public transit.
options.C: The conference center is far from campus.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering transportation, so accepting gratefully is the natural and polite response.

questions.stem: Are your office hours only on Tuesdays, or can I come in another day?

options.A: Tuesdays are my regular hours, but we can schedule another time if needed.
options.B: Office hours are for all students.
options.C: I usually have meetings on Thursdays.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about flexibility in office hours, so answering directly about alternatives is helpful.

questions.stem: The snowstorm last night made the roads impossible. I barely made it to campus.

options.A: I'm glad you got here safely. Several students emailed that they're stuck.
options.C: I live on campus, so I don't have to commute.

questions.explanation: The speaker is describing a difficult commute due to weather, so expressing relief for their safety and providing relevant context is empathetic.

questions.stem: I'd like to volunteer at the community center this semester. How do I sign up?

options.A: You can register at the service learning office or fill out the online form.
options.B: Volunteering looks good on a resume.
options.C: The community center hosts many events.

questions.explanation: The speaker wants to volunteer and is asking how to sign up, so providing clear registration options directly answers their question.

questions.stem: The map says the science building is east of the quad, but I think I'm going in circles.

options.A: You're almost there. Just keep walking past the fountain and turn left.
options.C: The science building was renovated recently.

questions.explanation: The speaker is lost and asking for directions, so giving clear navigational guidance is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: Something urgent came up. Could we move our meeting from 2 PM to 4 PM?

options.A: No problem. I'll see you at 4 PM in the same room.
options.B: You should manage your time better.
options.C: I have another meeting at 4 PM too.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking to reschedule, so confirming the new time and place is the cooperative response.

questions.stem: I'm number five on the waitlist for Biology 201. What are my chances of getting in?

options.A: Students usually drop within the first week, so your chances are fairly good.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about their likelihood of getting into a waitlisted course, so providing realistic information about typical waitlist movement is helpful.

I'm driving to the conference center. Do you need a lift?
Are your office hours only on Tuesdays, or can I come in another day?
The snowstorm last night made the roads impossible. I barely made it to campus.
